'''Ben Harrison''' (born 24 February 1988)<ref name="WW">{{Cite web|title=Team: Forwards – Ben Harrison|url=http://warringtonwolves.com/team/?section=forwards|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160122034232/http://warringtonwolves.com/team/?section=forwards|archive-date=2016-01-22|access-date=2021-05-17|website=Warrington Wolves}}</ref> is a former [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|lf|pr}} and {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Barrow Raiders]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]]. He has played at international level for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]], [[England national rugby league team|England]] and the [[England Knights]].

He previously played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]] and on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the Super League.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Ben Harrison was born in [[Barrow-in-Furness]], [[Cumbria]], England, he is the younger brother of the rugby league footballer; [[Liam Harrison (rugby league)|Liam Harrison]], and he was a pupil, and played [[rugby union]], at [[Chetwynde School]] in [[Barrow-in-Furness]].
[[File:Ben Harrison Warrington.jpg|thumb|left|Harrison playing for the Warrington Wolves in 2008]]

==Playing career==
===Warrington Wolves===
Harrison signed for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in 2005, he made his début for the Warrington side against the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], and other than two [[Loan (sports)|loan]] periods, he played his entire club career for the Warrington club.

Harrison spent a month on [[Loan (sports)|loan]] at the [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] club during 2007.<ref>{{cite web|last=Flanagan|first=Chris|title=Widnes spell served Ben well|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/whereilive/1387175.widnes_spell_served_ben_well|work=Warrington Guardian|publisher=Newsquest|access-date=25 May 2014|date=12 May 2007}}</ref>

Harrison played in [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]'s 25–16 victory over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in the [[2009 Challenge Cup Final]] at [[Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 29 August 2009, in front of a crowd of 76,560, he played in the 30–6 victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[2010 Challenge Cup Final]] at [[Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 28 August 2010, in front of a crowd of 85,217,<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8938067.stm|title=Leeds 6–30 Warrington|publisher=BBC|date=28 August 2010|access-date=22 November 2019|first=Ged|last=Scott}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |location=London |work=The Guardian |title=Chris Hicks hat-trick leads Warrington to Challenge Cup triumph over Leeds |first=Andy |last=Wilson |date=28 August 2010 |access-date=22 November 2019<!--DASHBot--> |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100831105934/http://www.guardian.co.uk/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |archive-date=31 August 2010 |url-status=live |df=dmy }}</ref> and he played in the 35–18 victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] club in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] at [[Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 25 August 2012, in front of a crowd of 79,180.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web
 |url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012! 
 |date=25 August 2012 
 |work=Warrington Wolves Official Site 
 |access-date=20 November 2019
 |url-status=dead 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |archive-date=17 December 2013 
 |df=dmy 
}}</ref>

Harrison played in Warrington's 18–26 defeat by [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] at [[Old Trafford]], [[Manchester]] on Saturday 6 October 2012, in front of a crowd of 70,676,<ref>{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=6 October 2012|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref> and he played in the 16–30 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]] at [[Old Trafford]], [[Manchester]] on Saturday 5 October 2013, in front of a crowd of 66,281.<ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington v Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131007163603/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|url-status=dead|archive-date=7 October 2013|work=BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=Guardian |access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

Harrison played four matches on [[Loan (sports)|loan]] at the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] during 2016.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

===Barrow Raiders===
On 11 September 2020 it was announced that Harrison had signed with [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] for the 2021 season<ref>{{cite web|website=www.loverugbyleague|date=11 Sep 2020|access-date=26 Dec 2020|title=Ben Harrison signs for hometown club Barrow|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ben-harrison-signs-for-hometown-club-barrow/}}</ref>

In October 2022, Harrison was served with a four-year ban for an anti-doping rule violation after testing positive for [[Drostanolone]] while training with Barrow Raiders in April 2021.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63320528 |title=Ben Harrison: Ex-Warrington Wolves forward given four-year doping ban |work=BBC News |date=19 October 2022}}</ref> The ban ended in June 2025.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/23061608.rugby-league-player-ben-harrison-given-four-year-drugs-ban/ |title=Rugby league player Ben Harrison given four-year drugs ban |work=Warrington Guardian |date=19 October 2022 |first=Matt |last=Turner}}</ref>

==International==
Ben Harrison represented [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Ireland|Ireland]] in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].
